displayName: Poland – Student Visa (National D + Residence for Studies)
shortDescription: Polish Embassy / VFS · UdSC (Office for Foreigners) · D visa · University acceptance · Proof of funds

policyAlert.summary: Confirm requirements on www.gov.pl before quoting.

alert.title: Residence permit after arrival
alert.body: After entry, apply for temporary residence permit at Polish Embassy / VFS within required deadline.

kpis.label: Government fee
kpis.sub: + proof of funds required living funds

about.label: Description
about.value: National visa (Type D) for study at a recognised Poland university. After entry, apply for temporary residence permit.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: University admission · Proof of funds · Health insurance · Motivation letter · Valid passport · Language proficiency as required

about.label: Proof of funds
about.value: €800/month (verify UdSC amount). Verify current amount on official portal before quoting.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: Polish Embassy / VFS · UdSC (Office for Foreigners)

about.label: After approval
about.value: Enter Poland; register address; apply for residence permit within visa validity.

eligibility.criterion: Proof of funds for study period
eligibility.note: Verify current amount on official portal
English or local language per program

redFlagsBanner: After refusal, address embassy feedback before rebooking—proof of funds and admission letter cannot be rushed last minute.

redFlags.title: Proof of funds insufficient
redFlags.description: Proof of funds below €800/month (verify UdSC amount) or inconsistent bank history.
redFlags.fix: Open blocked account or show €9,600+ equivalent with 3–6 month bank history before embassy slot

redFlags.title: Insufficient proof of funds
redFlags.description: Amount below required monthly sum or not from recognised provider.
redFlags.fix: Open proof of funds at approved bank for full required amount

redFlags.title: Admission not from recognised institution
redFlags.description: Private unrecognised college or institution not listed for international students.
redFlags.fix: Verify institution on www.gov.pl/web/diplomacy before collecting fees

redFlags.title: Weak motivation letter
redFlags.description: Generic letter not explaining study plan and Poland choice.
redFlags.fix: Course-specific motivation linking prior study to Polish program

redFlags.title: Language proof missing
redFlags.description: Program requires Polish/English certificate not provided.
redFlags.fix: Obtain required IELTS/TOEFL or Polish certificate per admission

faqs.q: Can we guarantee student visa approval?
faqs.a: No. Embassy decides based on admission, funds, and credibility. Never promise approval.

faqs.q: What is proof of funds for Poland?
faqs.a: Typically €800/month (verify UdSC amount) months (€9,600+/year) living funds or equivalent. Verify current amount on www.gov.pl before quoting.

faqs.q: Proof of funds amount for 2026?
faqs.a: Typically €800/month (verify UdSC amount) (€9,600+/year). Verify current amount on www.gov.pl before quoting.

faqs.q: Can spouse accompany?
faqs.a: Dependants may apply separately; prove additional funds and accommodation. Assess case-by-case.

faqs.q: English-taught programs need Polish?
faqs.a: Depends on program—follow admission and embassy requirements.

faqs.q: How long to convert to residence permit?
faqs.a: Apply at Migration Department (www.gov.pl) soon after arrival and university registration.

faqs.q: Can I work on student visa?
faqs.a: Limited working hours permitted—verify current rules for students in Poland.

faqs.q: What after graduation?
faqs.a: Discuss Graduate job-search temporary residence permit (verify on www.gov.pl) separately.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: National visa (Type D) for study at a recognised Polish university. After entry, apply for temporary residence permit at Migration Department (www.gov.pl).

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: University admission letter · Proof of funds (€800/month (verify UdSC amount)) · Health insurance · Motivation letter · Valid passport · Language proficiency as required

faqs.q: What is Proof of funds for this service?
faqs.a: €800/month (verify UdSC amount) months (€9,600+/year) typically required unless scholarship or sponsor alternative applies.

faqs.q: Important note on Proof of funds?
faqs.a: €800/month (verify UdSC amount) months (€9,600+/year) typically required unless scholarship or sponsor alternative applies.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: Polish Embassy/Consulate in India · VFS Global Poland · Migration Department (www.gov.pl) after arrival

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: Enter Poland; register address; apply for temporary residence permit at Migration Department (www.gov.pl) within visa validity.

faqs.q: Is Admission from recognised Polish institution required?
faqs.a: Admission from recognised Polish institution is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Proof of funds (€800/month (verify UdSC amount)) required?
faqs.a: Apply early—processing takes weeks

faqs.q: Is Proof of funds or financial proof required?
faqs.a: €9,600+/year or equivalent with 3–6 month bank history

faqs.q: Is Health insurance (travel + statutory) required?
faqs.a: Assess whether Health insurance (travel + statutory) is satisfied for this case.

faqs.q: Is Motivation letter and CV required?
faqs.a: Motivation letter and CV is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Language proficiency (as required) required?
faqs.a: Polish or English per program

faqs.q: Is Valid passport required?
faqs.a: Valid passport is typically required.

faqs.q: What is the Processing time?
faqs.a: 6–12w — After complete file complete

faqs.q: What is the Government fee?
faqs.a: €75 — + proof of funds required living funds

faqs.q: What is the Our approval rate?
faqs.a: 88% — ~74% benchmark

faqs.q: What is the Required docs?
faqs.a: 15 — + proof of funds €9,600+

faqs.q: What is the Consultancy fee?
faqs.a: See fee tab — + govt & third-party

faqs.q: What if the client has Proof of funds insufficient?
faqs.a: Open blocked account or show €9,600+ equivalent with 3–6 month bank history before embassy slot

faqs.q: Why is Proof of funds insufficient a problem?
faqs.a: Proof of funds below €800/month (verify UdSC amount) or inconsistent bank history.

faqs.q: What if the client has Insufficient proof of funds?
faqs.a: Open proof of funds at approved bank for full required amount

faqs.q: Why is Insufficient proof of funds a problem?
faqs.a: Amount below required monthly sum or not from recognised provider.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
Proof of funds funds belong to client—never commingle with firm accounts
proTips: Confirm proof of funds and admission letter 3–4 months before intended intake
Open proof of funds only after admission confirmed
Book embassy slot when file 90% ready
Motivation letter must match admission course
Verify health insurance accepted by embassy
postApproval: Guide on Migration Department (www.gov.pl) appointment after arrival
Remind proof of funds monthly withdrawal rules

timeline.title: Proof of funds, insurance, document compilation

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Expanded quiz to 75 levelled questions (25 per level).

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Proof-of-funds and admission timing matter—do not promise intake without confirming €9,600+ equivalent.

quiz.question: Indian students generally need:
options: Only passport
admission documentation + admission + proof of funds
Job offer
EU citizenship
quiz.explanation: Proof of funds €9,600+/year mandatory for student visa.

applicant.summary: Long-stay national student visa for Poland allows limited part-time work (typically up to 20 hours/week or per local law) after residence permit is issued.
details: Verify exact hours with local immigration office after arrival.
Cannot rely on work income alone for residence renewal.
restrictions: Work not allowed until residence permit issued in many cases

spouse.summary: Spouse/partner may apply for family reunion; work rights depend on Poland residence permit conditions.
details: Often requires proof of accommodation, integration, and sufficient income of sponsor.
